I have two parallel tasks that need the same level of care and accuracy.

First, I will provide batches of raw information that must be entered into well-structured spreadsheets. All formulas are already set up; your job is to copy the values into the correct rows and columns, remove any inconsistencies, and hand the file back in a clean Google Sheets format (Excel is fine as long as nothing breaks on import).

Second, I need short business texts translated between English and the following languages: Spanish, French, German, and Arabic. The tone must stay professional and any glossary terms I supply have to be respected. I expect a human review for fluency and correctness—machine output alone will not meet the mark.

Deliverables 
• Updated spreadsheet for each data batch, error-free 
• Translated documents in editable Word or Google Docs with mirrored formatting 
• Brief translator’s note highlighting any ambiguous phrases

Acceptance criteria 
• No misplaced or missing entries in the sheet (spot-checks will be run) 
• Consistent, natural-sounding translations with all key terms correctly rendered 
• Turnaround time: 48 hours per batch

full_description:
I run a small but growing YouTube channel built around meditation, relaxation and mindful lifestyle themes, and I’m ready to hand the week-to-week production workload to a reliable specialist. The channel’s audience comes to us for calming, high-quality nature visuals that help them unwind, so everything we publish needs to look pristine, flow peacefully and feel utterly soothing. 

Your core rhythm will be simple: two vertical Shorts every day and one longer video each week. Shorts should stay under 60 seconds, be formatted 1080 × 1920, and deliver quick moments of serenity—gentle waterfall clips, drifting clouds, rippling lakes, sunrise horizons, that kind of vibe. The weekly long-form upload (around 8-10 minutes) can weave multiple scenic shots together, optionally layered with a calm and soothing voice-over if you feel narration enhances the atmosphere. 

I’ll supply a folder of raw 4K footage and royalty-free audio; you’ll handle the rest—editing in Premiere Pro or Final Cut, colour grading, subtle sound design, tasteful text where useful, custom thumbnail creation (Canva or Photoshop is fine), keyword research, SEO-friendly titling, tagging and scheduled publishing. Consistency matters more than anything: each upload should look and feel like part of the same tranquil brand. 

Deliverables 
• 2 YouTube Shorts uploaded and scheduled daily 
• 1 long-form relaxation video uploaded and scheduled weekly, with custom thumbnail, title, description and tags 
• End-of-month analytics snapshot highlighting retention, CTR and any optimisation ideas 

All videos must be copyright-safe and rendered in at least 1080p (4K preferred for the long form). If you already maintain nature or meditation channels, share links—seeing your previous mood-matching edits will help me decide faster. I’m aiming for a long-term partnership, so show me how you can keep the channel’s peaceful pulse beating day after day without missing a step.

full_description:
I have a complete set of architectural DWG drawings and need them translated into a clean, well-structured BIM model in Autodesk Revit. The target is LOD 300, so the geometry must accurately represent walls, doors, windows, floors, roofs, and other architectural components with correct dimensions, layer naming, and family assignments, but it does not yet require fabrication-level detail.

Key points 
• Source material: architectural DWGs only. 

• Software: Revit is mandatory. 

• Level of detail: LOD 300 – elements placed in their precise locations, with basic metadata (type, size, material) filled in. 

Walls should be modeled using their actual layer composition whenever possible (not as generic single-layer walls), including the main architectural materials and finishes.

Doors and windows should be created using appropriate Revit families with accurate dimensions and key parameters (type, size, material, comments where applicable).

 Room s and levels should be properly defined so that room schedules and area calculations can be generated automatically.

 The model should be suitable for generating plans, elevations, sections, 3D views, schedules, and quantity takeoffs directly from Revit.

* Revit schedules for rooms, doors, windows, wall areas, floor areas, ceiling areas, and roof areas.
* A brief summary PDF highlighting any assumptions, missing information, or conflicts identified during modeling.


• The model should remain fully editable, using standard Revit families and best BIM practices, so it can be further developed in future project phases.

The objective is to create a high-quality BIM model that will serve as the project’s master architectural model for future structural, MEP, visualization, and quantity takeoff development.

Title

Migrate & Match ~300 Product Photos from Old Website to New Shopify Store (60 Products)

Category

Data Entry / Web Design / Shopify

Budget Type

Fixed Price (recommend getting quotes) or Hourly — your call based on bids


Project Description

I have a new Shopify store built out with product pages already created (structure, titles, descriptions, and collections are done). I need someone to migrate product photos from my old, currently-live website over to the correct products on the new Shopify site.

Scope of work:


~60 products total
Each product has an average of 5 photos, so approximately 300 images need to be downloaded from the old site and uploaded/matched to the correct product on Shopify
Photos must be matched accurately to the correct product and collection (product names/SKUs will line up between old and new site — I'll provide a mapping reference or access to both sites to cross-check)
In addition to product photos, there are a handful of remaining (non-product) pages on the new website that still need content/images filled in — I'll provide details on these once we start


What I'll provide:


Login/access to the new Shopify admin
Link to the old live website (all photos are publicly viewable there)
A product list / mapping guide showing which old-site item corresponds to which new Shopify product
Any brand assets or guidelines needed for the remaining pages


What I'm looking for in a freelancer:


Experience working inside Shopify admin (uploading images to products/variants, organizing media)
Strong attention to detail — this is a matching task, so accuracy matters more than speed
Comfortable downloading/organizing images in bulk and keeping them named/sorted clearly
Bonus: basic photo cleanup skills (cropping, background consistency) if some images need minor touch-ups before upload


Deliverables:


All ~300 photos correctly uploaded and matched to their corresponding Shopify products (correct product, correct order/placement)
Confirmation of any old-site products/photos that don't have a clear match on the new site (flagged for my review)
Remaining website pages populated with provided content/images


Timeline: Please include your estimated turnaround time in your bid.

full_description:
Residential Fire Sprinkler Designer Needed – California Project

We are seeking an experienced residential fire sprinkler designer to prepare a complete, permit-ready fire sprinkler design for a residential project located in California.

Project Scope

The designer will be responsible for:

>Reviewing the architectural and plumbing plans
>Designing a complete residential fire sprinkler system
>Preparing sprinkler head locations and coverage
>Preparing sprinkler piping layout and pipe sizing
>Performing hydraulic calculations
>Reviewing the available domestic water supply, pressure, and flow
>Determining whether a dedicated fire service, pump, tank, backflow preventer, or other equipment is required
>Coordinating the sprinkler system with ceilings, beams, soffits, HVAC, lighting, plumbing, and structural elements
>Preparing riser details, system notes, legends, schedules, and installation details
>Responding to plan-check comments and revising the plans until approval

Design Requirements

The design must comply with all applicable requirements, including:

>California Residential Code
>California Fire Code
>NFPA 13D
>Local city, county, and fire district requirements
>Current California-adopted codes and local amendments


Required Deliverables

>Permit-ready residential fire sprinkler plans
>Sprinkler head layout
>Complete piping layout with pipe sizes
>Hydraulic calculations
>Water-demand calculations
>Riser diagram
>Equipment and material specifications
>General notes, details, and code references
>PDF drawing set
>Editable CAD files, preferably AutoCAD DWG
>Revisions required by the local fire department or permitting authority

Qualifications

Applicants should have:

>Proven experience designing residential fire sprinkler systems in California
>Strong knowledge of NFPA 13D and California code requirements
>Experience submitting plans to California fire departments and local jurisdictions
>Experience performing hydraulic calculations
>Familiarity with AutoSPRINK, HydraCALC, SprinkCAD, AutoCAD, Revit, or equivalent software
>Ability to provide examples of previously approved California residential sprinkler plans

Please identify the California-licensed professional or contractor who will be responsible for signing or approving the plans, where required by the local jurisdiction. A California C-16 Fire Protection Contractor license or other applicable California design credential is preferred.

Information Requested With Proposal

Please provide:

1. Your relevant California residential sprinkler design experience
2. Examples of similar permit-approved projects
3. Software used for design and hydraulic calculations
4. California license or certification information, if applicable
5. Expected turnaround time
6. Number of plan-check revision rounds included
7. Confirmation that editable CAD files and hydraulic calculation files will be provided

The selected designer must coordinate closely with the architect, plumbing designer, and property owner throughout the permitting process.

I’m commissioning an original, trademark-ready cartoon seagull that will become the core character for a new line of T-shirts. The first milestone is a clean, standalone illustration of the bird itself—no props, no background—so I can lock down the trademark application. Once that base design is approved, I’ll need additional versions of the same seagull in action poses for Surfing, Basketball, Soccer and, over time, most other popular sports.

The look I’m after is light-hearted and instantly likeable: think bold outlines, expressive eyes and a style that translates well to screen printing at various sizes. • Master character artwork in editable vector format (AI or EPS) plus a high-resolution transparent PNG

• 100 % original work that passes a reverse-image search 
• Consistent line weight, color palette and proportions across every pose 
• Ready for print at 300 dpi with colors limited to a practical screen-printing palette

full_description:
Residential Infinity Pool and 4-Person Hot Spa Hydraulic and Mechanical Design

We are looking for an experienced swimming pool mechanical, plumbing, or hydraulic engineer to prepare a complete fluid mechanical design for a residential infinity-edge swimming pool with an integrated or adjacent heated spa for approximately four people.

Project Overview

The project is located in California, USA and includes:

>Residential infinity-edge swimming pool
>Approximate pool size: 40 ft × 10 ft
>Heated square spa for approximately four adults
>Preliminary spa size: approximately 7 ft × 7 ft, subject to final design
>Infinity-edge catch basin or surge tank
>Dedicated pool mechanical equipment room
>Pool and spa filtration, circulation, heating, sanitation, drainage, and automatic control systems
>Slat pool cover 

Architectural drawings, pool layout, elevations, site information, and the proposed mechanical-room location will be provided to the selected freelancer.

Scope of Work

The selected engineer or designer will be responsible for preparing a complete hydraulic and mechanical design, including the following:

1. Calculate the required pool and spa circulation flow rates.
2. Determine appropriate turnover rates based on applicable residential pool standards and local requirements.
3. Design the swimming pool circulation and filtration system.
4. Design the infinity-edge overflow system, catch basin, and surge tank system.
5. Calculate the required catch-basin or surge-tank capacity.
6. Size all piping, including:
>Pool suction piping
>Pool return piping
>Main drains
>Skimmer piping
>Infinity-edge overflow piping
>Catch-basin piping
>Spa suction and return piping
>Spa jet piping
>Spa air piping
>Fill-water piping
>Drain and waste piping
> Filter backwash piping, if applicable

7. Calculate total dynamic head, pressure losses, pipe velocities, and pump operating points.
8. Select and size the following equipment:
>Pool circulation pump
>Infinity-edge or catch-basin pump
>Spa circulation pump
>Spa hydrotherapy jet pump
>Spa air blower
>Pool and spa filters
>Pool heater
>Spa heater
>Sanitization equipment
>Chemical-treatment equipment
>Automation and control equipment
>Valves, check valves, unions, flow meters, and associated accessories

9. Design the four-person heated spa, including:

>Recommended finished internal dimensions
>Water depth
>Seating height and bench layout
>Approximate water volume
>Number and location of hydrotherapy jets
>Jet flow rate and pressure requirements
>Dedicated jet-pump sizing
>Air-blower sizing
>Suction fitting locations
>Return fitting locations
>Drain and overflow provisions
>Heater capacity
>Required heat-up time
>Operating temperature controls
>Spa insulation recommendations

10. Design separate operating modes for:

>Pool circulation
>Pool heating
>Spa circulation
>Spa heating
>Spa hydrotherapy jets
>Spa air blower
>Pool-to-spa changeover
>Spa spillover, if applicable
>Infinity-edge operation
>Maintenance or service mode

11. Prepare a valve and control diagram showing how the system changes between pool and spa operation.

12. Review the proposed mechanical-room location and recommend the most suitable equipment arrangement.

13. Prepare a mechanical-room layout showing:

>Equipment dimensions
>Pipe routing
>Valve locations
>Service clearances
>Access requirements
>Floor drainage
>Ventilation requirements
>Heater combustion-air and exhaust requirements, if applicable
>Electrical and control-panel locations
>Chemical-storage considerations

14. Coordinate the design with the architectural, structural, electrical, waterproofing, landscape, and pool-construction drawings.

15. Identify required pool and spa safety provisions, including:

>Anti-entrapment protection
>Dual-drain requirements
>Approved suction fittings
>Vacuum-release protection, if required
>Check valves
>Temperature limits
>Emergency shutoff requirements
>Overflow and drainage protection

Required Deliverables

The final design package should include:

>Pool hydraulic calculations
>Spa hydraulic calculations
>Flow-rate calculations
>Turnover calculations
>Total dynamic head calculations
>Pipe-sizing calculations
>Pipe-sizing schedule
>Pump-selection calculations
>Equipment schedule
>Heater-sizing calculations
>Spa heat-up calculation
>Infinity-edge overflow calculation
>Catch-basin or surge-tank volume calculation
>Pool and spa piping plans
>Spa jet and air piping layout
>Mechanical-room equipment layout
>Complete piping schematic or P&ID
>Valve and control operating diagram
>Equipment specifications
>Installation notes
>CAD drawings
>PDF drawings
>Editable hydraulic calculation files, preferably Excel
>Final coordinated design suitable for contractor pricing and permit submission

Design Requirements

The design should be:

>Practical to construct
>Energy-efficient
>Easy to operate
>Easy to maintain and service
>Compatible with the available equipment-room space
>Coordinated with the pool and spa elevations

Suitable for California climate and construction practices
The designer should recommend the final spa dimensions. As a preliminary basis, the spa may be approximately 7 ft × 7 ft and designed for four adults, but the final size should be based on the seating layout, jet placement, water volume, structural requirements, and hydraulic calculations.
Preferred Qualifications

>Licensed mechanical engineer or experienced swimming pool hydraulic designer
>Previous experience with residential infinity pools
>Previous experience designing heated spas and hydrotherapy jet systems
>Familiarity with California swimming pool and spa requirements
>Knowledge of applicable anti-entrapment and pool-safety requirements
>Experience with Pentair, Jandy, Hayward, or equivalent equipment
>Proficiency in AutoCAD or similar CAD software
>Ability to provide previous design examples

Information Required in Your Proposal

Please include:

>Your relevant pool and spa design experience
>Examples of infinity-pool hydraulic designs
>Examples of heated-spa or hydrotherapy-system designs
>Your proposed design approach
>A complete list of included deliverables
>The design software you will use
>The number of revisions included
>Your expected completion schedule
>Your fixed-price quotation
>Whether the drawings and calculations will be signed or stamped
>Whether you are licensed to provide engineering services in California
>Any information or drawings you will require from us before beginning

full_description:
I need a turnkey procurement service for a project in Saudi Arabia that calls for both plastic and wood artifactual plants. All items will be sourced in or around Paris and packed into a single 20-ft container bound for Jeddah.

Your scope begins with locating the right stock, confirming visual and material quality, and then moves straight through to export documentation and final dispatch. Quality inspection on-site in France is critical; I want photo evidence, basic dimension checks, and confirmation that colours, finishes, and flame-retardant specs match the purchase order. 

Once the goods pass inspection, I expect you to coordinate everything that follows: pick-up scheduling, container stuffing, liaising with the chosen ocean carrier, and managing the customs process at both ends until the shipment is released in Jeddah.

Deliverables
• Supplier shortlist and confirmed purchase orders 
• Photo/video inspection report with measurements and any certifications 
• Packing list, commercial invoice, certificate of origin, bill of lading draft for my approval 
• Proof of container sealing and hand-over to the carrier 
• Status updates through to customs clearance in Jeddah and final arrival notice 

If you already work with reliable forwarders or have in-house brokerage, let me know—speed and accuracy on the paperwork will be the deciding factors.

full_description:
About Us

We are developing BidMasterPro, a SaaS platform and Chrome Extension that helps Freelancer.com users save time by generating AI-powered proposals, managing bids, and improving their bidding workflow.

We are looking for a reliable and proactive Remote Digital Marketing Assistant to help grow our online presence, increase free trial signups, and convert users into paid subscribers.

This is a long-term opportunity for the right candidate.

Responsibilities

Your daily tasks will include:

Create and publish social media content (LinkedIn, Facebook, X, Instagram)
Create simple graphics using Canva
Write blog articles using AI tools with proper editing
Upload blog posts to our website
Create YouTube Shorts from product demos
Schedule social media posts
Research keywords and SEO opportunities
Monitor competitors and report new ideas
Engage with comments and messages on social media
Assist with email marketing campaigns
Research online communities where our target audience is active
Reach out to bloggers, influencers, and partners for collaboration
Maintain a content calendar and weekly marketing reports
Suggest new marketing ideas to improve subscriptions
Requirements
Good written English
Experience with digital marketing
Basic SEO knowledge
Experience using Canva
Familiarity with ChatGPT or other AI tools
Experience with WordPress is a plus
Experience with social media management
Strong research skills
Organised and able to meet deadlines
Self-motivated and able to work independently
Bonus Skills
Video editing (CapCut or similar)
Email marketing
Google Analytics
Google Search Console
Chrome Extension marketing
SaaS marketing experience
Knowledge of Freelancer.com or freelancing platforms
Daily Deliverables

Each working day you will typically:

Publish 3–5 social media posts
Create 1 short video or YouTube Short
Write or improve one blog article
Research keywords and competitors
Record completed work in a shared spreadsheet
Submit an end-of-day report summarising completed tasks
Working Hours
Remote
Flexible schedule
Part-time or Full-time (depending on experience)
Long-term position
To Apply

Please include:

A short introduction about yourself.
Your digital marketing experience.
Links to social media accounts or campaigns you have managed.
Any SEO or content writing experience.
Your expected monthly salary or hourly rate.
The AI tools you regularly use.
Your availability (hours per week).
Start your proposal with the words "Growth Partner" so we know you have read the complete job description.

We are looking for someone who enjoys learning, takes initiative, and wants to grow with our company. If you perform well, there will be opportunities for long-term work, bonuses, and increased responsibilities.

I already have a clear release and promotion plan mapped out—I simply need someone experienced enough to carry it through each month.

Your core responsibilities will be two-fold:

• Playlisting & socials 
 – Curate and update themed playlists on Spotify, Apple Music and YouTube Music in line with my release calendar. 
 – Post and schedule engaging content across Instagram, TikTok and Twitter, keeping the brand voice consistent and growing followers organically.

• Video creation & design 
 – Produce a steady stream of music videos, short promotional cuts and lyric videos. 
 – Mix animated elements, live-action footage and abstract art when it suits the track, handling editing, motion graphics and final colour.

You’ll receive my step-by-step rollout guide; I simply want you to follow it and deliver on time. Most of the raw assets—audio masters, branding elements, rough storyboards—are ready for you.

Deliverables each month:
1. Two fresh, fully optimised playlists
2. A weekly social media pack (caption + artwork or short clip)
3. One main video (music, promo or lyric) plus any cut-downs/re-formats needed for socials

The role is set at a flat $200 retainer per month, renewed if results match the benchmarks outlined in the plan.

If you know your way around playlist algorithms, Adobe CC / Final Cut / DaVinci, and the latest social trends, I’d love to see examples of your past work and discuss how soon you can start.

full_description:
CASE SUMMARY
Jurisdiction: SDNY
Defendants: A, B, C, D (roles in the offense, lowest to highest: A, B, C, D)
Cooperation/Acceptance Posture:
A: Confessed; did not cooperate
B: Did not confess or cooperate
C: Attempted cooperation; did not receive a 5K1.1 letter
D: Received a 5K1.1 letter

Sentencing History:
2018: A, B, and C sentenced to life imprisonment. One of their counts carried a statutory mandatory minimum of life.

2020: D sentenced to a non-life term. At D's sentencing, the judge expressly tied D's sentence to the life sentences of A, B, and C, stating words to the effect of "A, B, C got life, so why shouldn't D get life" — and included this reasoning as part of the § 3553(a) analysis.

2019: United States v. Davis decided by the Supreme Court.

2022: Vacatur order issued for certain charges against A, B, and C as a result of Davis, including the count that had carried the statutory mandatory minimum of life. Davis did not affect D's charges, because D was convicted of different offenses. However, D's offenses of conviction include all of the conduct of A, B, and C as relevant conduct.

2022: A resentenced to a non-life term.

2024: B resentenced to a non-life term.

2025: C resentenced to a non-life term.

At each of the A, B, and C resentencings, the government and Probation continued to advocate for life sentences.

Issue Presented:
D now seeks resentencing. Questions to answer:
1) What is the substantive argument for resentencing D, given that D's original 2020 sentence was explicitly anchored to the (since-vacated) life sentences of A, B, and C?
2) What case law supports this argument?
3) What is the timeliness argument for relief under 28 U.S.C. § 2255, given that D was sentenced in 2020 and the last co-defendant resentencing concluded late 2025? 
4) After filing a 2255 a stay needs to be requested because the direct appeal of C is outstanding, what is the case law to cite?
